observations: RATIFIED (AT5 test-defect correction, precedent 013/019/026): the original AT5 required the FULL .agent-stripped scratch sweep to report 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'. Independently confirmed this over-specified into an out-of-scope, infeasible claim: shipped docs that C9 sweeps by default (docs/architecture/ADR-001, ADR-002; methodology/00_planning.md, 03_circuit_breaker.md, 04_tool_driven_state.md; registry/scope_definition_patterns.md; templates/AGENTS.md; schema/README.md) name concrete .agent/run_state.json / .agent/feature_list.json / .agent/product_spec*.md paths (verified by grep against the live tree) that only exist in the populated framework tree, so C9 legitimately FAILs once .agent/ is stripped -- unrelated to C11 and non-goaled ('No edit to any C1-C10 check function logic'). The corrected AT5 asserts exactly and only feature_list_004 AT5's literal wording ('C11 still reports PASS and the sweep does not fail on C11's account'): [C11] PASS present + [C11] FAIL absent. This is the genuine C11 skip-if-absent assertion, NOT a weakened one -- it is retained in full; only the spurious coupling to C9's out-of-scope failure was dropped. Legitimate correction.

RATIFIED (implementation-internal fix 4a): check_c11_dogfood_payload_skip is present and returns a designed disk-free counted PASS; the disclosed transient 'missing definition' is resolved -- not test-weakening.

RATIFIED (implementation-internal fix 4b): the --target C11 line format ships as '[C11] {PASS|FAIL} dogfood --target routed: <route>' rather than the contract narrative's '(--target, routed: <route>)' parenthetical. This is required to satisfy AT7's 'routed: contract$' end-anchored grep (the parenthetical would end in 'contract)' and fail the anchor). The acceptance tests (AT6/7/8) are the source of truth and are unchanged; the message string was conformed to them. Not test-weakening.

AT5 scratch-copy reproduction could not be re-run directly by the validator (the compound cd/copy scratch-build command was denied by the sandbox permission system). The two load-bearing facts were instead verified independently: (a) C11 skip-if-absent -> PASS is structurally certain from check_c11_dogfood_sweep (each family existence-guarded by os.path.isdir/isfile; absent family contributes zero errors -> exit 0 -> [C11] PASS), and (b) the C9 references were confirmed present in the live shipped docs by grep. The generator's captured evidence .agent/evidence/028-verify-05.txt corroborates: C9 FAIL (12 unresolved .agent/ refs), [C11] PASS, SUMMARY: 10 passed, 1 failed.

AH-4 green tree independently reproduced: default 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' (exit 0, [C9]/[C10]/[C11] all PASS); --payload 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed' (exit 0, [C11] PASS trivially); all three FAIL fixtures reject with exit 1 and the correct routes ([C11] FAIL routed: qa_verdict / contract / run_state); non-regression confirmed -- --target NIZAM.json -> [C4] PASS (exit 0), --target tools/fixtures/broken_index.json -> [C4] FAIL (exit 1), neither routed to C11.

Scope fidelity (git diff HEAD -- . ':(exclude).agent'): exactly tools/validate.sh (M, strictly additive: 4 new functions + print_usage docs + 3 registration call-sites, one of which reroutes the *.json call-site from check_c4_index to check_c11_or_c4_target) plus 3 new fixtures under tools/fixtures/ (invalid_qa_verdict.json [qa_pass discriminator], invalid_contract.json [contract_id discriminator, status='drafting' non-enum], invalid_run_state.json [circuit_breaker/scope_budget discriminators, missing 'history']). Nothing else outside .agent/. check_c4_index's own function body is byte-unchanged (not present in the diff). Tracked .agent changes are orchestrator-owned only (.agent/feature_list_004.json wording reconciliation, .agent/run_state.json state) -- no historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact touched; validate.sh contains zero references to .agent/validator/. All non-goals held (no change to verify_lib.sh, NIZAM.json, CHANGELOG/CONTEXT/README/bootstrap/schema/workflows).

final_verdict.summary: Mode-B APPROVED. C11 implementation is a faithful, strictly-additive realization of approved contract 028 across all three modes: default sweep (content-routed .agent/qa via restricted {review,qa_pass|verdict} rule with 027_failures.json correctly skipped; .agent/contracts and run_state validated by-path), --target FULL-rule dispatcher (routes recognized shapes to C11, falls 'none' through to the UNMODIFIED check_c4_index -- NIZAM.json and broken_index.json still reach C4), and --payload disk-free trivial counted PASS. All 8 Mode-B checks pass (B1-B8), scope is exactly tools/validate.sh + 3 tools/fixtures/ files, and the green tree reproduces (default 11/0, payload 10/0). The disclosed AT5 verification-command correction is RATIFIED as a legitimate test-defect fix (original over-specified an out-of-scope C9 failure into the assertion; corrected form tests the genuine, unweakened C11 skip-if-absent behavior per feature_list_004 AT5's literal wording), and the two implementation-internal fixes (payload_skip definition; routed:-anchored message format for AT7) are ratified as in-scope implementation details, not test-weakening. Zero blocking issues.
